response.justification: The candidate has genuine geospatial domain experience through multiple GIS platforms and a FOSS4G talk, which is relevant to the industry context. However, the role's primary requirement is production-grade modern C++ or Rust, and the candidate's entire career is built on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. The only C/C++ exposure is roughly six months at National Instruments over a decade ago, with no Rust listed at all. There is also no evidence of routing pipeline work, tiling systems, or navigation guidance solutions — the core technical scope of the role.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial platform development across multiple projects (Northwind Labs, GISCollective, Green Map GIS) demonstrates real domain familiarity
Distributed systems experience handling millions of HTTP requests per second at Dunnhumby with low-latency async I/O patterns
Consistent engineering discipline — TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development — across multiple senior roles
concerns: No modern C++ depth: only ~6 months of C/C++ work at National Instruments over a decade ago; Rust is entirely absent from the resume
Primary technical stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang — not systems-level languages suited for high-performance routing tile pipelines
Zero experience with routing data pipelines, navigation guidance systems, or map tile optimization, which are the core deliverables of this role
